fields.label: URL
fields.description: Specify a complete HTTP URL (for example http://your_server:8080)

fields.label: With Credentials
fields.description: Whether credentials such as cookies or auth headers should be sent with cross-site requests.

fields.label: With CA Cert
fields.description: Needed for verifying self-signed TLS Certs

fields.label: Forward OAuth Identity
fields.description: Forward the user's upstream OAuth identity to the data source (Their access token gets passed along).

fields.label: Allowed cookies
fields.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials. Both fields are used together when root.basicAuth is true.

TLS mutual auth: enabling jsonData.tlsAuth reveals the ServerName input and the Client Cert / Client Key secret slots in @grafana/ui TLSAuthSettings.tsx. All three are required for a working mTLS handshake.
Self-signed / custom CA verification: enabling jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert requires the CA certificate PEM in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.

OpenTSDB-specific behaviour knobs. tsdbVersion (1..4 mapping to <=2.1, ==2.2, ==2.3, ==2.4) drives response parsing and rate-option shape (pkg/opentsdb/utils.go:138,225-266; src/datasource.ts:187). tsdbResolution (1=second, 2=millisecond) controls the msResolution flag on outgoing queries (src/datasource.ts:178-180). lookupLimit caps the /api/search/lookup response size for tag-value autocomplete (pkg/opentsdb/callresource.go:361; src/datasource.ts:230).

instructions.msg: OpenTSDB authentication is a direct-toggle model — not a discriminator field. root.basicAuth and root.withCredentials are independent boolean switches rendered by @grafana/ui DataSourceHttpSettings (src/components/ConfigEditor.tsx:15-20); jsonData.oauthPassThru is a third independent switch inside HttpProxySettings. All three can be true simultaneously. Only when root.basicAuth is true does the editor render the User (root.basicAuthUser) + Password (secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword) inputs. There is no visible 'No Authentication' option — leaving every switch off is the No-auth mode. The plugin's Go backend never inspects these fields directly; it delegates everything to the SDK's settings.HTTPClientOptions(ctx) (pkg/opentsdb/opentsdb.go:29-37).

Minimal No-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:4242","jsonData":{"tsdbVersion":1,"tsdbResolution":1,"lookupLimit":1000}}. Minimal Basic-auth payload: {"url":"http://opentsdb.example.com:4242","basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<user>","jsonData":{"tsdbVersion":1,"tsdbResolution":1,"lookupLimit":1000},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<password>"}}. Minimal OAuth-forward payload: {"url":"http://opentsdb.example.com:4242","jsonData":{"tsdbVersion":1,"oauthPassThru":true}}. root.url is required — pkg/opentsdb/opentsdb.go:47 reads settings.URL directly and the CheckHealth probe issues an api/suggest?q=cpu&type=metrics GET against it (pkg/opentsdb/opentsdb.go:87-138).

TLS is independent of the auth toggles. jsonData.tlsAuth=true enables mTLS and reveals the ServerName input plus Client Cert / Client Key secret textareas in @grafana/ui TLSAuthSettings; provisioning must supply jsonData.serverName, secureJsonData.tlsClientCert, and secureJsonData.tlsClientKey for the SDK to build a valid mTLS transport. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true enables server-cert verification against a custom CA and requires secureJsonData.tlsCACert. Both can be enabled together; the TLSAuthSettings block only renders when either is true. jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true bypasses verification entirely and should only be used for testing. All TLS fields are consumed by settings.HTTPClientOptions(ctx) inside NewDatasource (pkg/opentsdb/opentsdb.go:29).

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.basicAuthPassword == true) instead of secureJsonData. Custom HTTP headers are modeled by the jsonData_httpHeaders array field using an indexedPair storage mapping: each header's name is written to jsonData.httpHeaderName<N> and its value is written write-only to secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue<N> (starting at N=1), exactly as the @grafana/ui CustomHeadersSettings component and the SDK HTTPClientOptions expect. The per-header httpHeaderValue<N> secrets are dynamic and are therefore not listed among the static SecureJsonDataKeys.

OpenTSDB-specific storage: jsonData.tsdbVersion is a numeric enum stored as a JSON number (1 → '<=2.1', 2 → '==2.2', 3 → '==2.3', 4 → '==2.4'; default 1 per src/datasource.ts:60 and src/components/OpenTsdbDetails.tsx:37). The backend Go type is float32 (pkg/opentsdb/opentsdb.go:66) — Version 4 triggers array-response parsing (pkg/opentsdb/utils.go:138,247-254) and adds ?arrays=true to /api/query. jsonData.tsdbResolution is 1 (second, default) or 2 (millisecond); resolution 2 sets msResolution=true on outgoing query bodies (src/datasource.ts:178-180). jsonData.lookupLimit is an int32 that caps /api/search/lookup responses for keyvalue autocomplete (pkg/opentsdb/callresource.go:361); default 1000 per src/datasource.ts:62. All three are stored as JSON numbers.

Connection quirks: OpenTSDB expects a base URL such as http://host:4242 (default TSD port). The plugin constructs endpoint paths by joining the base URL with /api/query, /api/suggest, /api/aggregators, /api/config/filters, and /api/search/lookup (pkg/opentsdb/opentsdb.go:100, pkg/opentsdb/callresource.go). Do not include a trailing /api segment in root.url — path.Join will still work but the SDK's URL validator flags trailing slashes. A URL that parses but points at a non-OpenTSDB backend passes admission but fails CheckHealth (which requires a 200 from /api/suggest). Note: jsonData.lookupLimit is written by an <Input type="number"> whose onChange stores event.currentTarget.value (a string) — see the upstream findings in the README; the backend expects int32 so the raw value may fail unmarshal after the first edit.
